name: cpig-kin-15-watt-straight-line
description: kin-15 Watt straight-line linkage — James Watt's 1784 4-bar parallel motion. Planar 4-bar (chassis + crank_left + coupler + crank_right) producing an APPROXIMATE vertical straight line at the coupler midpoint M. Operator-noted regression value: 'the straight line is an approximation, not exact — visual diff should show the slight curve at the extremes.' Same topology family as kin_11 (single closed loop) but with the Watt-specific 60°/120° crank angles + 40 mm short coupler. Forward-dynamics integrates 2s under 250 N·mm constant left-crank torque; Canary's per-frame scrub (Session B+) steps AnimSlider through 11 values to animate the GIF. Phase 4.6.F.6 — fills the original prompt's deferred kin_15 slot.

setup.vlmDescription: James Watt's 1784 straight-line 4-bar linkage shown as a bound mechanism at a single time-step of a 2-second forward-dynamics integration. Three bound bodies are rendered as thin pipes: a slightly-thicker A–M–B coupler triangle (M is the trace midpoint) and two thinner rocker arms — O→A (left crank, pivoting from world origin) and P→B (right crank, pivoting from (100,0)). At slider=0 the design pose has both cranks pointing inward and upward at 60°/120° from world +X. As the slider advances, a constant 250 N·mm torque on the left crank rotates the linkage; the coupler midpoint M traces an APPROXIMATE vertical straight line. A faint design-pose skeleton (frame triads at O and P, joint circles at A, B, M) is overlaid via Preview Diagram in Display Mode 2.
